steps.title: Find what the evidence must establish
steps.content: The passage claims that energy expense prevents full-speed strategies even in escape contexts. The blank must be filled with data proving that animals use less than maximum speed when escaping—the highest-stakes scenario where energy constraints would seem least tolerable.

steps.title: Identify the choice matching that pattern
steps.content:
The graph shows that multiple lizard species use 30–40%, 40–50%, 50–60%, 70–80%, and 80–90% of maximal speed when escaping—that's at least 5 species averaging below 90%.

Option B captures this directly: if animals don't reach full speed even under predation pressure, it demonstrates that energy costs prevent maximal deployment.

Options A, C, and D either show similar percentages across contexts without addressing the energy constraint, suggest high-speed use predominates (contradicting the claim), or focus on pursuit rather than escape.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
